payload.claim_text: Farebox recovery ratio has a long-term focus. It includes costs that are not funded in the current year, except in an accounting-ledger sense, but are, in effect, passed on to future years. Those costs include depreciation and interest on long-term debt. Approximately 20% (and sometimes more) of MTA costs are not recovered in the current year from farebox revenues, other operating revenues or subsidies. That is why MTA operating statements generally show deficits. In addition, the recovery ratio allocates centralized MTA services to the Agencies, such as Security, the costs of the Inspector General, Civil Rights, Audit, Risk Management, Legal and Shared Services.
payload.subject: Farebox recovery ratio
payload.description: Definition of the farebox recovery ratio and its long-term focus

payload.claim_text: Farebox operating ratio focuses on Agency operating financial performance. It reflects the way MTA meets its statutory and bond-covenant budget-balancing requirements, and it excludes certain costs that are not subject to Agency control, but are provided centrally by the MTA.
payload.subject: Farebox operating ratio
payload.description: Definition of the farebox operating ratio focusing on Agency operating financial performance

tool_args.label: Revised methodology for LIRR and MNR

payload.description: LIRR and MNR farebox operating and recovery ratio calculations in committee agenda materials use a revised methodology that produces different statistics than this table
payload.missing_information: The LIRR and MNR ratios presented in committee agenda materials use a revised methodology to put the railroads on a more comparable basis, and those statistics differ from the statistics presented in this table
